external_indicators.detail: US foreign aid contraction directly threatens LMIC consulting demand. USAID priority geographies overlap with Acasus footprint (Pakistan, Afghanistan, sub-Saharan Africa).
UK aid budget frozen at 0.5% GNI (down from 0.7%). FCDO is a likely Acasus funder given UK registration and LMIC focus.
Requires rare combination: field experience in LMICs + data/analytics skills + consulting pedigree. 22% headcount growth YoY signals hiring pressure.

external_indicators.indicator: Big 4 / MBB entry into impact consulting
external_indicators.detail: McKinsey, BCG, Deloitte all expanding social impact practices. Compete for same government and multilateral contracts.
Growing market overall, though Acasus LMIC niche is a small slice dependent on donor funding cycles.

external_indicators.indicator: AI readiness for data-driven reform delivery
external_indicators.detail: Budapest data engineering team (24 people) with AWS, Tableau, ETL pipelines. Positioned to absorb AI tooling for government analytics.

internal_indicators.detail: Founder Fenton Whelan stepped to Chair in 2025. New CEO Jonny Barty (internal promote) still establishing. Transition during growth phase adds execution risk.
Aid-funded consulting = lumpy contracts. Loss of 1-2 major government programs could materially impact $35M revenue base.
Below average. Field deployment in conflict/post-conflict zones drives burnout risk. Could affect retention of scarce talent.
Strong growth signal. Expanding from ~217 to ~265 staff in 12 months. Confirms pipeline health.

external_indicators.detail: ACARA publishes per-school NAPLAN results on MySchool. CSPD schools show rising academic outcomes — critical for Uplift evidence-based teaching framework validation.
ACARA MySchool data shows Catholic systemic schools average 14-16 students per teacher. CSPD 43,000 students / ~4,500 staff suggests ~9.5:1 total staff ratio but teaching ratio will be higher.
Index of Community Socio-Educational Advantage scores vary across CSPD 80 schools. Western Sydney includes growth corridors and lower-SES areas. Shapes funding allocations.

external_indicators.indicator: Commonwealth + NSW recurrent funding per student
external_indicators.detail: Government funds ~75% of Catholic school operating costs. Needs-based funding model (Gonski 2.0) via CSNSW. Stable but politically sensitive.
One of Australia fastest-growing corridors. Drives enrolment demand but also capex pressure for new school builds and expansions.
NSW Education Standards Authority requirements tightening post-Royal Commission. Mandatory reporting, Working With Children checks, data governance obligations all increasing.
CSPD mid-market pricing is a structural advantage as cost-of-living pressures push families from independent to Catholic system.

internal_indicators.detail: System-wide evidence-based teaching initiative. Success depends on teacher adoption, data infrastructure, and coaching capacity across 80 schools.
Hired from analogous role at CECG. Signals investment in learning outcomes technology and data infrastructure as 2026 priority.
Explicit leadership appetite for data-driven improvement but current systems likely fragmented across 80 schools. Integration challenge.
Australia-wide teacher shortage acute in Western Sydney. Affects class sizes, specialist subject availability, and staff wellbeing.

external_indicators.detail: 20.23% CAGR. Young population (median age ~25), underserved provincial demand, government subsidies driving growth.
Commission on Higher Education controls degree-granting authority, program approval, and tuition increase caps. Compliance cost but also barrier to entry.
Direct competitor in Philippine affordable HE. iPeople 74,000 enrollment is less than half PHINMA. Differentiated by STEM focus and Mapua brand.
Listed on Philippine Stock Exchange. Revenue PHP 5.32B FY2024 (~$95M). Public market accountability drives governance discipline.
Large addressable market for tertiary education. Population growth + rising middle class = sustained enrollment demand.

external_indicators.indicator: Board exam pass rates as quality signal
external_indicators.detail: Mapua University is top engineering school. Pass rates are the primary quality benchmark in Philippine HE market.

internal_indicators.detail: Thin margins on PHP 5.32B revenue. PHP 351M net income leaves little buffer for investment in technology or expansion.
74,000 students growing at 10% annually. Pipeline from K-12 programs feeds into HE institutions.
Joint ownership by two large conglomerates. Strategic alignment risk if parent priorities diverge.

external_indicators.detail: Dominant position in UK HE operations software. 350+ institutional clients. Near-saturation means growth must come from ARPU or geography.
180M+ bed-nights/year managed. UK PBSA is a 50B+ GBP asset class with institutional investment driving professionalization and software demand.
Generic PropTech platforms (Yardi, RealPage) could move into education vertical. Kinetic moat is domain expertise but faces platform scale disadvantage.
OfS (Office for Students) increasing scrutiny on student welfare, accommodation standards, and data handling. Creates compliance demand that benefits Kinetic.

external_indicators.indicator: SaaS transition / cloud migration in UK HE
external_indicators.detail: Universities moving from on-prem to cloud. Kinetic SaaS model aligns with institutional procurement trends.

internal_indicators.detail: Small team for dominant market position. Capacity constraints on product development, support, and international expansion.
85% UK penetration means growth requires international markets. Different regulatory and market dynamics in each country.
Cross-sell across product lines within existing customer base. Student engagement module is newest growth vector.

external_indicators.detail: $154M in 2025 projected to $847M by 2034. Provincial markets remain significantly underpenetrated.
PACUCOA Level III is the highest accreditation level. Creates structural barrier for competitors and validates quality positioning.

external_indicators.indicator: Free HE Act / UAQTEA tuition subsidies
external_indicators.detail: Government subsidy covers tuition for students at accredited institutions. Policy reversal would directly impact affordability for PHINMA target market.
PHINMA University of Pangasinan ranked #1 nursing school nationally. 9 of Top 10 passers. Self-reinforcing brand flywheel.
PHINMA Araullo University named Top 1 performing school nationwide for August 2025 Criminology Licensure Exam.
177,851 students across 3,500-5,000 FTE (not all teaching). Bare-bones philosophy suggests lean staffing. Faculty quality critical for board exam results.
KKR Global Impact Fund II. Digital transformation is explicit partnership mandate. Validates growth thesis and funds acquisition pipeline.
High for affordable segment. Quasi-subscription revenue model depends on retention. Each point of retention = ~1,780 students.
Ambitious Indonesia expansion via Horizon University (Karawang) and Kalbis University (Jakarta). JV structure adds complexity.

internal_indicators.detail: Strong profitability for education sector. 14% YoY revenue growth. Disciplined cost control.
Proprietary PHINMA Ed Learning App deployed but basic (scheduling, attendance, grades). CIO in place. KKR mandate includes digital transformation.
Provincial markets have scarce licensed healthcare educators. Board exam performance depends on faculty quality — critical constraint for growth.

external_indicators.detail: 11-19% CAGR. 250M+ out-of-school children globally, concentrated in SSA. Massive unmet demand.
Series A $4.25M (2024) from impact investors. Total cumulative $6-10M+. Sector attracting serious development capital.
Rori AI tutor delivered via WhatsApp reaches 150,000+ users. Zero app-install friction. WhatsApp is the platform in West Africa.
Sierra Leone, Liberia, Ghana, Rwanda. Government contracts provide scale but add political risk and payment delays.
Low-cost private school chains face political backlash in some African markets. Rising B-Corp / evidence-led positioning differentiates but sector risk exists.
One of few SSA operators with rigorous evidence base. Learning gains validated through randomized controlled trials. Critical for donor/government credibility.

internal_indicators.detail: Estimated $2-8M total budget. Trending toward earned revenue but still dependent on grants and philanthropic capital.
300,000+ students. Growth outpacing infrastructure. Quality consistency across 900 sites in 4 countries is the central operational challenge.
WhatsApp-native AI tutoring is a genuine product differentiator. 150,000+ users. Question is whether it can drive earned revenue.

external_indicators.detail: Australian charitable giving is stable but competition for donor dollars is intense. Cost-of-living pressures may reduce discretionary giving.
Annual Gala Ball is the primary fundraising event. Single-event concentration creates significant revenue volatility risk.
Operates 10 schools in rural Uganda. Education policy changes, land tenure, and foreign NGO regulations are ongoing risks.
SFL model provides dramatically better ratios. 4,500 students across 250 staff = ~18:1 total ratio. Strong differentiation from government alternative.
One of the world youngest populations (median age ~16). Sustained demand for education. Question is funding capacity to serve it.
Revenue raised in AUD, costs incurred in UGX. Currency movements directly affect purchasing power and operational budget.

internal_indicators.detail: Small but deep. Holistic model (primary + secondary + vocational) over 17 years. Growth constrained by fundraising capacity.
Management span across two continents with very different operating contexts. Coordination overhead for a small org.

internal_indicators.value: Low — concentrated in events + major donors
internal_indicators.detail: Estimated $2-5M annual. Gala Ball + small number of major donors = moderate concentration risk. Limited recurring/institutional funding.

external_indicators.detail: QF is state-backed, funded by hydrocarbon endowment. Provides operational stability but creates single-funder dependency.
WISE is part of Qatar knowledge economy and soft power strategy. Continued relevance depends on Qatar strategic priorities for global education influence.
UNESCO, World Bank, OECD, ASU+GSV, HundrED all compete for education innovation mindshare. WISE differentiates on policy + Global South focus.
Called Nobel Prize for Education. Key brand asset. 12,000+ cumulative summit attendees. 35,000+ community members.
AI in education is the dominant theme in global education policy. WISE positioned to convene this conversation but needs to lead, not follow.

internal_indicators.detail: Operational funding entirely from QF. Zero earned revenue diversification. Strategic alignment with QF leadership is existential.
Very small team for global ambition. Limits capacity for year-round programming, research output, and community engagement.
Convening impact is hard to measure. ROI narrative to QF depends on soft metrics (attendees, media mentions, community size) rather than hard outcomes.
